verses.text: They lead Him to Pilate; Art thou the King of the Jews? 'Thou sayest it.' Quiet confession. He owns His kingship before the governor.
They lead Him to Pilate; Art thou the King of the Jews? 'Thou sayest it.' Quiet confession. He owns His kingship before the governor.
They lead Him to Pilate; Art thou the King of the Jews? 'Thou sayest it.' Quiet confession. He owns His kingship before the governor.
Pilate finds no fault; they grow fierce, 'He stirreth up the people.' False charge. Innocence is met with louder accusation.
Pilate finds no fault; they grow fierce, 'He stirreth up the people.' False charge. Innocence is met with louder accusation.
Hearing He is a Galilaean, Pilate sends Him to Herod. Passed along. Men shift the burden of judging the Holy One.
Hearing He is a Galilaean, Pilate sends Him to Herod. Passed along. Men shift the burden of judging the Holy One.
Herod, hoping for a miracle, gets no answer; he mocks Him and sends Him back. Silent before scorn. Christ gives no sign to idle curiosity.
Herod, hoping for a miracle, gets no answer; he mocks Him and sends Him back. Silent before scorn. Christ gives no sign to idle curiosity.
Herod, hoping for a miracle, gets no answer; he mocks Him and sends Him back. Silent before scorn. Christ gives no sign to idle curiosity.
Herod, hoping for a miracle, gets no answer; he mocks Him and sends Him back. Silent before scorn. Christ gives no sign to idle curiosity.
Herod, hoping for a miracle, gets no answer; he mocks Him and sends Him back. Silent before scorn. Christ gives no sign to idle curiosity.
Pilate finds nothing worthy of death; 'I will... chastise him, and release him.' Half-measure. He would compromise rather than do right.
Pilate finds nothing worthy of death; 'I will... chastise him, and release him.' Half-measure. He would compromise rather than do right.
Pilate finds nothing worthy of death; 'I will... chastise him, and release him.' Half-measure. He would compromise rather than do right.
Pilate finds nothing worthy of death; 'I will... chastise him, and release him.' Half-measure. He would compromise rather than do right.
They cry, 'Away with this man, and release... Barabbas,' a murderer. Tragic choice. They prefer a killer to the Saviour.
They cry, 'Away with this man, and release... Barabbas,' a murderer. Tragic choice. They prefer a killer to the Saviour.
They cry, 'Away with this man, and release... Barabbas,' a murderer. Tragic choice. They prefer a killer to the Saviour.
Pilate would release Jesus, but their voices 'prevailed,' Crucify him. Mob fury. The clamour of the crowd overrides justice.
Pilate would release Jesus, but their voices 'prevailed,' Crucify him. Mob fury. The clamour of the crowd overrides justice.
Pilate would release Jesus, but their voices 'prevailed,' Crucify him. Mob fury. The clamour of the crowd overrides justice.
Pilate would release Jesus, but their voices 'prevailed,' Crucify him. Mob fury. The clamour of the crowd overrides justice.
He gives sentence as they require, freeing Barabbas and yielding Jesus to their will. Justice surrendered. Pilate sacrifices the innocent to please the crowd.
He gives sentence as they require, freeing Barabbas and yielding Jesus to their will. Justice surrendered. Pilate sacrifices the innocent to please the crowd.
Simon bears the cross; women bewail Him; 'weep... for yourselves, and for your children.' Coming sorrow. He pities even those who mourn Him.
Simon bears the cross; women bewail Him; 'weep... for yourselves, and for your children.' Coming sorrow. He pities even those who mourn Him.
Simon bears the cross; women bewail Him; 'weep... for yourselves, and for your children.' Coming sorrow. He pities even those who mourn Him.
The days come when the barren are called blessed; 'if they do these things in a green tree, what... in the dry?' Worse to come. If the innocent so suffers, what of the guilty?
The days come when the barren are called blessed; 'if they do these things in a green tree, what... in the dry?' Worse to come. If the innocent so suffers, what of the guilty?
The days come when the barren are called blessed; 'if they do these things in a green tree, what... in the dry?' Worse to come. If the innocent so suffers, what of the guilty?
Crucified between two thieves, He prays, 'Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do.' Praying for foes. From the cross He intercedes for His murderers.
Crucified between two thieves, He prays, 'Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do.' Praying for foes. From the cross He intercedes for His murderers.
Crucified between two thieves, He prays, 'Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do.' Praying for foes. From the cross He intercedes for His murderers.
Rulers and soldiers mock, 'If thou be the Christ, save thyself'; the title reads, THE KING OF THE JEWS. Scorned King. They jeer the kingship that is truly His.
Rulers and soldiers mock, 'If thou be the Christ, save thyself'; the title reads, THE KING OF THE JEWS. Scorned King. They jeer the kingship that is truly His.
Rulers and soldiers mock, 'If thou be the Christ, save thyself'; the title reads, THE KING OF THE JEWS. Scorned King. They jeer the kingship that is truly His.
Rulers and soldiers mock, 'If thou be the Christ, save thyself'; the title reads, THE KING OF THE JEWS. Scorned King. They jeer the kingship that is truly His.
One thief rails; the other rebukes him, 'this man hath done nothing amiss.' Faith at the cross. A dying robber sees what the crowd could not.
One thief rails; the other rebukes him, 'this man hath done nothing amiss.' Faith at the cross. A dying robber sees what the crowd could not.
One thief rails; the other rebukes him, 'this man hath done nothing amiss.' Faith at the cross. A dying robber sees what the crowd could not.
'Lord, remember me'; 'To day shalt thou be with me in paradise.' Saving grace. The penitent thief is welcomed in his final hour.
'Lord, remember me'; 'To day shalt thou be with me in paradise.' Saving grace. The penitent thief is welcomed in his final hour.
Darkness covers the land; the veil is rent; 'Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit'; He dies. Willing death. He lays down His life in trust to the Father.
Darkness covers the land; the veil is rent; 'Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit'; He dies. Willing death. He lays down His life in trust to the Father.
Darkness covers the land; the veil is rent; 'Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit'; He dies. Willing death. He lays down His life in trust to the Father.
The centurion glorifies God, 'Certainly this was a righteous man'; the people smite their breasts. Witness and grief. The cross moves both Gentile and crowd.
The centurion glorifies God, 'Certainly this was a righteous man'; the people smite their breasts. Witness and grief. The cross moves both Gentile and crowd.
The centurion glorifies God, 'Certainly this was a righteous man'; the people smite their breasts. Witness and grief. The cross moves both Gentile and crowd.
Joseph, a good man, begs the body and lays it in a new sepulchre. Honoured burial. A secret disciple gives Him an honourable grave.
Joseph, a good man, begs the body and lays it in a new sepulchre. Honoured burial. A secret disciple gives Him an honourable grave.
Joseph, a good man, begs the body and lays it in a new sepulchre. Honoured burial. A secret disciple gives Him an honourable grave.
Joseph, a good man, begs the body and lays it in a new sepulchre. Honoured burial. A secret disciple gives Him an honourable grave.
The women behold the tomb, prepare spices, and rest the sabbath. Faithful love. They keep the day even while their hearts mourn.
The women behold the tomb, prepare spices, and rest the sabbath. Faithful love. They keep the day even while their hearts mourn.
